title: Sedum duckbongii
intro: Sedum duckbongii, also known as Korean Sedum or Jeju Stonecrop, is a perennial succulent plant native to the Korean island of Jeju. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, offering various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as arthritis, digestive issues, and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Sedum duckbongii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Korean medicine, Sedum duckbongii is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments for skin care.
taxonomy: Sedum duckbongii is classified as a species within the plant family Crassulaceae. This family is commonly known as the stonecrop or orpine family. Sedum duckbongii is further classified as a genus within the Crassulaceae family, the genus Sedum. It belongs to the order Saxifragales, a group of flowering plants that includes many species of stonecrops. The classification of Sedum duckbongii is also reflected in its binomial nomenclature, with the genus name Sedum referring to its classification within the Sedum genus.
